coding.display: Glucose mean value [Mass/volume] in Blood Estimated from glycated hemoglobin

note.text:
The eAG (Estimated Average Glucose) in 80% of patients should be within 15% of this calculated value.
The Estimated Average Glucose calculation is NOT VALID if:
1. Patient is not stable glycemia 
    (A1C change is less than 1% in last 6 months)
2. Patient is pregnant
3. Patient has ABNORMAL RBC LIFESPAN such as hemoglobinopathy 
   (e.g. SICKLE CELL DISEASE, blood loss or transfusion, chronic renal or liver disease, high-dose Vitamin C or erythropoietin or anemia)
Testing performed at Associated Physicians LLP, 4410 Regent Street, Madison, WI 53705

Patient Instructions

For post-concussive syndrome: I agree with all of the things you are doing. Other things to consider would be a medication like topiramate for headaches; other options would be amitriptyline, which is an antidepressant 
For blood pressure:

Patient Instructions

Start with 10 mg of nortriptylene at night 
Can increase to 20 mg at night after 1-2 weeks (allow your system to get used to it)

payload.contentString:
Topic: MyChart - Other

I'm trying to send a message to my care team but I get the following errors:

> Messaging is available to MyUnityPoint users after they have a scheduled appointment with a MyUnityPoint provider. After you have scheduled an appointment, your profile will be linked to the provider's care team and messaging will be available.

I have already completed an appointment with Dr. Dhillon, so it looks like something may be misconfigured?

Thanks for helping me get this turned on!

Josh

topic.text: RE: "Message Your Care Team" not working

payload.contentString:
Hi Joshua,

I've sent your request to message Dr. Dhillon to our Advance Support to review.  These may take a few days for a response.  Please contact your provider via phone if needing assistance before you hear back from me.

Sincerely,
Gayla
MyUnityPoint Technical Support

For additional support, please feel free to contact the MyUnityPoint support team by calling 877-224-4430 Monday - Friday 8am to 5pm.

----- Message -----
   From: Joshua C. Mandel
   Sent: 8/20/2018 12:17 PM CDT
     To: Patient Customer Service Request Mailing List
Subject: "Message Your Care Team" not working

Topic: MyChart - Other

I'm trying to send a message to my care team but I get the following errors:

> Messaging is available to MyUnityPoint users after they have a scheduled appointment with a MyUnityPoint provider. After you have scheduled an appointment, your profile will be linked to the provider's care team and messaging will be available.

I have already completed an appointment with Dr. Dhillon, so it looks like something may be misconfigured?

Thanks for helping me get this turned on!

Josh

topic.text: RE: "Message Your Care Team" not working

payload.contentString:
Good morning,

You should now be able to message Dr. Dhillon.  Please let me know if you have any questions.

Sincerely,
Gayla
MyUnityPoint Technical Support

For additional support, please feel free to contact the MyUnityPoint support team by calling 877-224-4430 Monday - Friday 8am to 5pm.

----- Message -----
   From: Joshua C. Mandel
   Sent: 8/20/2018 12:17 PM CDT
     To: Patient Customer Service Request Mailing List
Subject: "Message Your Care Team" not working

Topic: MyChart - Other

I'm trying to send a message to my care team but I get the following errors:

> Messaging is available to MyUnityPoint users after they have a scheduled appointment with a MyUnityPoint provider. After you have scheduled an appointment, your profile will be linked to the provider's care team and messaging will be available.

I have already completed an appointment with Dr. Dhillon, so it looks like something may be misconfigured?

Thanks for helping me get this turned on!

Josh

Hello Joshua

Dr Dhillon wanted you to know the lab test for H.Pylori (the bacteria that can cause stomach ulcers) is negative.

If you have any further questions or concerns, do not hesitate to call or send a My chart message.

Have a nice day,

Thank you,
Dawn, RN
608-233-9746

Josh,
I understand that you have questions about the criteria for the MRI vs CT of the head.  I called AIM Specialty Health which is the company your insurance carrier uses for prior authorizations of diagnostic imaging.  They cannot fax me a copy of the criteria but directed me to the clinical guidelines on their website.  I would advise you to look at the criteria for MRI of the brain and CT of the head on their website.  Please remember that the CT was approved, but MRI was not approved.  Please let us know what you want to do.
Thank you,
Mary Ann Picone, DNP

After reviewing the trade-offs, I would like to proceed with scheduling a self-pay MRI at Midwest Open MRI in Middleton, rather than pursuing a head CT. Can you please help me get an order in place for the MRI study?

Thanks,
Josh

Mary Ann Picone, DNP

I have faxed the brain MRI order to Midwest Open MRI. I was told by the scheduler that the out of pocket cost will be $650 and this includes the scan, disc of images, and the report will be sent to the referring provider. I was also told that they will give you a call to schedule this.

Take care,
Nicole, RN

I got a message here that my email address isn't working? [REDACTED-EMAIL-1] is my registered address and it should work, so it would be good to know if there is an issue here.

Thank you for following up in app messaging here. Over the course of the last week my wrist has gotten much better; there was no injury that I am aware of but I had sharp pain when applying pressure under the thumb and pretty restricted range of motion for a couple of days. By now things are nearly back to normal, with just a bit of dull pain on pressure. At no point was there tingling redness or smelling.

topic.text: RE:  Dr. Dhillon departure from Associated Physicians

payload.contentString:
January 21, 2021

 Re: Primary Care Physician Assignment

 Dear Valued Patient:

 I have made the difficult decision to leave my medical practice at Associated Physicians, to focus on my family. I have enjoyed my time at Associated Physicians and being your primary care provider.

 I understand that you may have concerns or questions about this transition. Associated Physicians is actively recruiting a new Internal Medicine physician to become your new primary care physician. In the interim, all plans of care will continue without disruption as one of my Internal Medicine partners will ensure your healthcare needs are met. We are developing plans to address outstanding appointments, insurance issues and billing. If you have an appointment scheduled after my departure date, one of our staff will reach out to reschedule. I know Associated Physicians will continue to provide excellent care to you and your family.

 Yours very truly,

 Puneet S. Dhillon M.D.

topic.text: Associated Physicians No Show Policy Update for All Patients

payload.contentString:
Josh,

 We appreciate you choosing Associated Physicians, LLP. We schedule our appointments so each patient receives the right amount of time to be seen by our providers and staff. That is why it is very important that you keep your scheduled appointment with us and arrive 15 minutes early to complete the check-in process.
 As a courtesy, we attempt to provide each patient with an appointment reminder (via text, phone, or email) to confirm their appointment one week prior to the appointment. A second appointment reminder is sent the night before the appointment. To facilitate this communication, we ask that you update us if any of your contact information is changed.

If your schedule changes, and you cannot keep your appointment, please contact us at (608) 233-9746 to reschedule your appointment as soon as possible; this allows us to accommodate patients who are waiting for an appointment. As a courtesy to our office, as well as the other patients who are waiting to schedule with our physicians, please call at least 24 hours before your scheduled appointment to cancel.

 If you do not come to your appointment, we may assess a $20 "no-show" service charge to your account. This "no-show" service charge is not reimbursable by your insurance company. You will be billed directly.

 If you do not come to your new patient/establish care visit, you may reschedule one more time with AP; however, you will not receive priority over other new patients that are already scheduled.
 We understand that unforeseen emergencies occasionally happen. If one of these events occurs prior to your appointment, please call the clinic at (608) 233-9746 to inform our staff as soon as you are aware of the impact on your appointment. The fee may be waived for a single event.
 After three consecutive no-shows to your appointment, our practice may terminate its relationship with you.

 While most patients will never see this fee, it is important that everyone understands the importance of attending all your appointments, and communicating with us in a timely manner if your schedule changes.

 We appreciate your understanding regarding this change to our practice. We enjoy caring for you and your family members.

The Providers and Staff at Associated Physicians

August 3, 2023

 Dear Mr. Mandel:

 Life is busy. We understand going to the doctor isnt always top-of-mind. But thats why were here, to help you stay on top of your health and wellness.

 We noticed youre due for the following:

 Annual Wellness Visit

 Weve made it easy to view your preventive care reminders and request an appointment with your provider  all in one click epichttp://healthmaint[here]. If youve already received this service, send us a short MyUnityPoint message, and well update your records.

 Keeping you healthy and happy is our top priority, and we look forward to hearing from you soon.

 Sincerely,
 Your UnityPoint Health Care Team

topic.text: PCP Out of Office July 1 - July 14

payload.contentString:
Hello all-

 I hope that everyone is doing well. I am writing to notify you that I will be out of the country for two weeks (from July 1- July 14). I will not be checking patient medical charts during this time, so there will be some lab results that come through that I will not see until I am back in Madison. Additionally, if there are non-urgent questions that come up during those two weeks, then I will not be able to see those questions until I return, so I recommend either holding on to them, or just being aware of the fact that I will not be replying until July 15th. My colleagues will be able to reply to all urgent items, and we have a fantastic nurse practitioner who can see you for urgent care matters (cough, sore throat, fevers, pain, etc.). I apologize for the inconvenience, but I did want to set expectations with all of you before I leave the country.

 Sincerely,
 Zoe Rammelkamp, MD

Hi Dr. Rammelkamp,

 Would you be able to order specific IgE blood tests for peanut and tree nut allergies? As a child, I experienced frequent hives, and skin testing by an allergist indicated allergies to peanuts and several tree nuts, including almonds, walnuts, hazelnuts, pecans, cashews, pistachios, and macadamia nuts. I have avoided peanuts and tree nuts since then, but I'd like to assess whether these allergies may have resolved.

Hi Josh- I don't think that we have that lab here. I am happy to refer you to Allergy Clinic and they can do the allergy testing. Let me know if interested. Thanks.

Thanks for the quick response! These are tests that LabCorp could do with an order for Test 604764 (CPT 86003(x9), details at https://www.labcorp.com/tests/604764/allergen-profile-nut-ige-with-component-reflexes). Josh- I'm sorry, but I would feel more comfortable if you had an allergist go through these things. I have literally never ordered these tests, nor sent them to LabCorp, so it is not in my comfort zone. Let me know if you want the referral though.

topic.text: Worth repeating a fasting lipid panel?

payload.contentString: I think it's probably worth repeating this fasting.

Hi Josh- overall these labs are fine. The triglycerides are elevated, which is likely related to not fasting. The LDL (bad cholesterol) is fine, as is the total cholesterol. The A1C (diabetes screen) is fine. If you would to come back fasting for the lipid panel, then let us know and someone can schedule a morning lab visit for you. Or, if you would like to wait till your next annual wellness visit, then that is fine; I would just recommend fasting for that visit.

December 15, 2025

Joshua C Mandel
 [REDACTED-ADDRESS-1]
 [REDACTED-ADDRESS-2]
Dear Valued Patient,

 I have made the difficult decision to leave my medical practice at Associated Physicians in order to pursue a career in clinical research. I have enjoyed my time at Associated Physicians and being your primary care provider. I will continue to see patients through March 5, 2026.

 I understand that you may have concerns or questions about this transition. Associated Physicians is actively recruiting a new physician to serve as my replacement, and an announcement will be made when the new physician is ready to schedule. In the meantime, my colleagues will address your acute concerns and medication refill requests. If you have an appointment scheduled after our departure date, one of our staff will reach out to reschedule.

 We appreciate your patience while we work to reschedule already scheduled visits and coordinate your preventative care. If you are unable to wait for the next available provider or if you prefer to transition your care to a different clinic, our staff will assist you in transferring records to your new clinic.

 I wish all of you the best. It has been an honor to serve as your doctor.

 Yours very truly,

 Zoe Rammelkamp, MD

Hi Josh,

I am sending you this message to your Mychart as the original message you sent did not go through your Mychart but instead was sent to "Mychart Administration" and then was forwarded to us from that department.

 I just wanted to let you know that I did try to call you in regards to scheduling an appointment with Dr. Fothergill but had to leave a message. Since I was unable to reach by phone, I went ahead and scheduled an appointment for you with Dr. Fothergill on the following date: 6/16/27 2:30 pm
Future AppointmentsDateTimeProviderDepartmentCenter6/16/2027 2:30 PMFothergill, Amy, MDAPLIMAssoc PhysAs you can see this appointment is scheduled for June of 2027. This is Dr. Fothergill's first available transfer of care appointment.
 If you need to reschedule, it is important that you call the clinic to do so. Our phone number if 608-233-9746. There are other options that are available with other physicians if you do not want to wait until 2027 and it is best that these options are discussed with a telephone conversation.

 Let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks
 Jess
